FT_IC1.evidence: Abstract explicitly states "Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development study" with baseline ages 8-11 years from 21 US sites (Sept 2016-Sept 2017).

FT_IC5.evidence: Cross-sectional observational study with multivariable linear mixed-effects models examining relationships between movement behaviors and global cognition.

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed publication in The Lancet Child & Adolescent Health (2018).

FT_IC2.evidence: Exposure is "recreational screen time" measured as part of a composite 24-hour movement guideline (physical activity + screen time + sleep) with no per-modality breakdown isolating phone or social media. Composite bundle without phone/SM isolation does not qualify under FT-IC2.

notes: Exclusion code: FT-EC2. Although cognition is individual-trait-level, the screen time exposure lacks phone/SM specificity.

FT_IC1.evidence: Study is embedded in "Amsterdam Born Children and their Development (ABCD) study, the Netherlands (n=2354)." This is the Dutch ABCD, not the U.S. ABCD Study. Methods section explicitly states this is a Dutch cohort.

notes: Exclusion code: FT-EC1. Cohort is Amsterdam ABCD (Dutch), not U.S. ABCD.

FT_IC1.evidence: Methods state "The ongoing ABCD Study collected demographic and screen use data (among other domains) from 11 878 children and primary caregivers at baseline (mean age = 9.9 years) beginning in 2016 with annual follow-up assessments" and uses ABCD propensity sampling weights from 21 sites.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: cross-sectional multivariable logistic regression examining associations between sociodemographic predictors and early smartphone ownership age.

FT_IC2.evidence: Smartphone ownership age is the outcome/DV, but all predictors (sex, race/ethnicity, household income, household structure, parental education) are demographic-only. No individual-trait-level variable (neural, behavioral, health, cognitive, well-being, personality, substance use, sleep, academic, etc.) appears on either side of any relationship. Demographic-only analysis fails FT-IC2.

notes: Exclusion code: FT-EC2. Paper examines sociodemographic correlates of early smartphone ownership, but lacks any individual-trait-level pairing required by FT-IC2.
